pass_summary: All five plan truths are implemented and verified in the diff. The core detection logic, runtime early-exit, and install-time skip are correct. One gap: the install-time skip path in configureStatusline() has no automated test coverage, and the early-return in the statusline action leaks stdin (Claude Code may stall waiting for the process to consume its piped data).

issues.title: Silent early-return before stdin drain may stall Claude Code
issues.description: In src/cli/commands/statusline.ts line 163, `if (!isStatuslineActive()) return;` exits before reading stdin. Claude Code pipes session JSON to the process; if the pipe buffer fills and the process exits without draining it, the parent may receive SIGPIPE or stall. This is especially relevant on slow machines or when the session JSON payload is large.
issues.suggested_fix: Drain stdin before returning: call `process.stdin.resume()` and `process.stdin.destroy()` (or simply read and discard the stdin chunks) before the early return, so the pipe is flushed cleanly. Alternatively, print an empty string (`process.stdout.write("")`) then drain stdin to signal normal completion.

issues.title: Install-time skip path has no automated test coverage
issues.description: Task 2's Definition of Done includes 'Install skips statusline config when another plugin is active' and 'Install logs informational message about skipped statusline'. The existing install.test.ts has no tests for the new conditional branch in configureStatusline() (the false-return path and the note() call at install.ts line 354-358). The plan explicitly lists these as DoD criteria.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests in src/cli/commands/install.test.ts that mock isStatuslineActive (or write a temp settings.json pointing to ccstatusline) and assert that configureStatusline() returns false and that settings.json is not overwritten.

issues.title: note() message references a config key that may not exist
issues.description: In install.ts line 356-358, the skip message tells the user to run 'sentinal config set statusline_override true'. If no such config key is implemented, this instruction is misleading. The plan does not mention a statusline_override config flag.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the 'Run sentinal config set statusline_override true to force.' clause from the note message, or implement the statusline_override config key.

truths.truth: isStatuslineActive returns false when settings.json command doesn't contain 'sentinal'
truths.evidence: statusline.ts line 61: `return command.toLowerCase().includes('sentinal')` returns false for 'npx ccstatusline-usage'. Test case 'should return false when command points to another plugin' confirms this.

truths.truth: isStatuslineActive returns true when settings.json command contains 'sentinal'
truths.evidence: statusline.ts line 61: includes('sentinal') returns true. Test case 'should return true when command contains sentinal' with '/Users/evan/.sentinal/bin/sentinal statusline' confirms this.

truths.truth: sentinal statusline outputs empty string when another plugin is active
truths.evidence: statusline.ts line 163: `if (!isStatuslineActive()) return;` — the action returns without writing to stdout, producing no output.

truths.truth: sentinal install claude skips statusline config when another plugin is detected
truths.evidence: install.ts lines 390-393: `if (!isStatuslineActive(settingsPath)) { return false; }` inside configureStatusline(), and the call site at line 352 wraps in `if (configureStatusline())` to skip the ok() message.

truths.truth: isStatuslineActive returns true when settings.json doesn't exist (safe default)
truths.evidence: statusline.ts line 42: `if (!existsSync(path)) return true;`. Test case 'should return true when settings.json does not exist' confirms this. The catch block also returns true for parse failures.
